name: Nsc (Naca Counseling Subsidiary) - N. Miami, FL
description_short: NACA offers character-based mortgage lending with no down payment, no closing costs, and no mortgage insurance, targeting low-to-moderate income and historically underserved borrowers.
description_long:
NACA (Neighborhood Assistance Corporation of America) was founded in 1988 by the Boston Hotel Workers Union as a housing advocacy organization and has evolved into a major mortgage lender. Over 36 years, NACA has served 3 million people and helped 500,000 become homeowners through 75,000 NACA mortgages, with $20 billion in mortgage commitments. The organization also provides 30% of all HUD housing counseling nationally, positioning itself as both a lender and a housing rights advocate.

NACA's primary offering is their "Best in America Mortgage" program, which features no down payment required, no closing costs or fees, no mortgage insurance, and no consideration of credit score in underwriting decisions. Mortgages are character-based, focusing on applicants' circumstances rather than traditional credit metrics. Current fixed rates as of April 2026 are 5.625% for 30-year, 5.125% for 20-year, and 5% for 15-year mortgages. The organization also operates "Achieve the Dream" events in multiple cities where prospective members can learn about qualification requirements and access services.

NACA distinguishes itself through its explicit focus on closing the racial wealth disparity gap and serving historically marginalized communities, particularly low-to-moderate income people and people of color. The organization combines mortgage lending with advocacy against predatory landlords and corporate real estate investors, and operates volunteer networks (NACtivist) alongside member services. Their character-based lending model directly contrasts with traditional credit score-dependent mortgage underwriting.

A significant caveat is that NACA requires members to actively participate in the organization and attend multi-day Achieve the Dream events to qualify for mortgages, which represents a higher barrier to entry than traditional lenders. Additionally, while rates are advertised as competitive, the website provides no detailed comparison to market rates or information about approval timelines, documentation requirements, or specific eligibility criteria beyond character-based assessment.
pros: No down payment required on mortgages, removing a major barrier to homeownership
No closing costs, fees, or mortgage insurance, reducing total borrowing costs significantly
Credit score is not considered in underwriting—character-based lending focuses on applicants' circumstances
Fixed-rate mortgages with below-market rates (5% for 15-year, 5.125% for 20-year as of April 2026)
Serves 30% of all HUD housing counseling nationally, providing comprehensive guidance
Explicit mission to serve low-to-moderate income and historically underserved communities, particularly communities of color
Member portal for document uploads, status tracking, and ongoing file management
cons: Requires active member participation and attendance at multi-day Achieve the Dream events as a condition of qualification, creating significant time commitment barriers
Website provides no information on approval timelines, processing speed, or typical loan approval rates
Limited transparency on specific credit requirements, income thresholds, debt-to-income ratios, or other underwriting criteria beyond 'character-based' assessment
No details on loan amounts, property type restrictions, geographic service areas, or refinancing options
Advertised rates lack context regarding market comparison, rate lock periods, or adjustability terms
best_for: Low-to-moderate income first-time homebuyers with limited savings for down payments
Borrowers with poor or limited credit history who don't qualify for traditional mortgages
People of color and historically marginalized communities experiencing systemic barriers to homeownership
Individuals willing to commit time to community involvement and multi-day qualification events
diagnosis: NACA is best for low-to-moderate income first-time homebuyers and historically underserved borrowers who cannot qualify for traditional mortgages due to limited credit history or down payment savings, and who are willing to invest time in community participation and multi-day events. The main caveat is that qualification requires active member engagement beyond standard mortgage application processes, and the website lacks transparency on specific underwriting criteria, timelines, and loan details needed to properly evaluate eligibility.
services: Character-based mortgage lending (no credit score consideration)
No closing costs or fees mortgages
Achieve the Dream multi-day qualification events
Member portal for document upload and case management
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
